summary: Iteration 3 thoroughly tested. All 10 new backend tests pass (placements list, polo/kids/hi-vis variants, multi-size checkout with exact £257.83 total, exclusivity 400, blank ignores placements, legacy path, unknown size, zero qty). Frontend product detail page (polo-shirt) fully exercised: colour swatches, size grid +/-/qty controls, size-summary (M×3 · L×2), placement exclusivity (left-breast ↔ full-front, full-front ↔ left/right breast), live price £74.95 with 2 placements then £44.95 when blank, buy-blank toggle disables placements (opacity-50 pointer-events-none) and zeros print cost, button text switches to 'Checkout (Blank)', customise-design correctly disabled when blank, network interception confirms /api/checkout/session called with correct {product_id, size_qtys, color, placements, blank} payload. Homepage shows price-promise-band (CTA→/contact) and price-promise-hero (CTA→/contact). Product page shows price-promise-card. ProductReviews filter chips render with correct counts (All 7, Photos 2, 5★4, 4★3) and click-filter works. 1 regression in legacy backend test surfaced.

minor.issue: When legacy payload sends quantity=0, server treats it as 1 (due to `int(payload.quantity or 1)` — Python truthiness). The old test_checkout_invalid_quantity expecting 400 now gets 200. Recommend changing to `int(payload.quantity) if payload.quantity is not None else 1` then explicit `if q_int < 1: raise 400`.

action_items: Fix legacy quantity=0 handling in /api/checkout/session — currently a quantity of 0 in the legacy single-size path is silently promoted to 1. Replace `int(payload.quantity or 1)` with explicit None check, then validate q_int >= 1 with 400.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py legacy quantity coercion `int(payload.quantity or 1)` masks 0 as 1 — small but real input-validation bug exposed by existing test.
ProductDetail.jsx is 376 lines — could split Section + price summary into sub-components but acceptable for now.
_validate_placements correctly enforces both directions of exclusivity at server level — defence in depth alongside FE.
PRODUCTS dict still inline in server.py; will be a maintenance hotspot when adding more product attributes.
seed_data_creation: Each Stripe checkout test creates a payment_transactions doc in MongoDB (initiated state, harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run all 3 backend test files together: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. The only failing test is backend_test.py::TestCheckout::test_checkout_invalid_quantity due to legacy quantity=0 promotion bug noted in action_items. New iteration 3 file is /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py.
rca of the issue: Legacy quantity=0 bug RCA: in server.py line ~373 `q_int = int(payload.quantity or 1)` — Python evaluates `0 or 1` to 1 because 0 is falsy. The subsequent `if q_int < 1` check is therefore unreachable for the 0 case. Reproduction: POST /api/checkout/session {product_id:'personalised-tee', quantity:0, size:'M', origin_url:'...'} → 200 instead of 400. Fix: change to `q_int = int(payload.quantity) if payload.quantity is not None else 1`.
